message:
test(fcp-core): pin Principal.kind serde + Provenance claim shape

Why
---
Two beads asked for "PrincipalKind" and "ProvenanceClaim" wire
contracts, but fcp-core does not expose public types literally
named those things. The closest analogues in the public surface
are:

  - PrincipalKind: the `Principal.kind` string field, documented on
    Principal as values such as "user", "agent", "service", and
    "webhook". Pinning the wire form here is more honest than
    inventing a type — it reflects what the JSON / CBOR actually
    look like to consumers reading enrollment, capability, and
    audit records.

  - ProvenanceClaim: the public Provenance struct that represents
    request- and object-level provenance claims (chain of
    ProvenanceStep, taint level, owning zone). Pinning the serde
    shape here locks in the field names and ordering that audit
    tooling and downstream attestation flows already depend on.

Both beads are from the bead-driven test-pin program that
systematically locks externally-visible serde and Display contracts
across fcp-core before they ship into broader use.

What
----
- crates/fcp-core/tests/principal_kind_serde.rs (new, 118 lines):
  - Pins JSON roundtrip for the four documented kind values
    (user, agent, service, webhook) plus optional display field
    handling, and verifies BTreeSet collisions match the documented
    kind set rather than silently expanding.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/provenance_claim_serde.rs (new, 180 lines):
  - Pins JSON + CBOR shape of Provenance with a representative
    ProvenanceStep chain, asserts that taint level, owning ZoneId,
    and step ordering survive the round-trip byte-for-byte, and
    pins error behaviour for malformed wire input.

Compatibility
-------------
Pure additions. No public types or signatures change. These tests
assert the status quo of the wire form rather than introducing a
new contract — if they ever start failing, that is the signal,
not the test itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
